content.full_text:
October 24, 1988


Honorable Newton R. Russell
Senator, 21st District
401 North Brand, Suite 424
Glendale, CA  91203

Re:  Our File No. I-88-397

Dear Senator Russell:

We have received your letter requesting that we draft a
reply, for you signature, in response to a letter from one of
your constituents.  The Fair Political Practices Commission is
a nonpartisan agency and therefore does not prepare
correspondence on behalf of members of the legislature or other
elected officials.  The Commission does provide advice or
information concerning application of the Political Reform
Act.  (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

The question presented by Michael More, your constituent,
concerns false and misleading advertising, a subject not
addressed by the Political Reform Act.  Thus, we are unable to
provide any specific answer about application of current law to
the Mr. More's concerns.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]
By:  Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

September 30, 1988


Senator Newton Russell
401 N. Brand Blvd. Suite 424
Glendale, CA  91203


Dear Senator:

All during this campaign, both on the Federal and State
level, I have heard the most outrageous attacks, half-truths,
and what must be out and out lies spoken by nearly everybody,
against everyone and everything else.

They all cannot be right, when you really examine the remarks
made, because they all contradict each other.  But by far,
the worst offender in this is the California State
Initiatives, their sponsors, and advertisements.  A strong
case in point is the flurry of attacks and counter-attacks
involving the many Insurance bills now before the voters.
They all claim that they are the only one, single bill that
will benefit the people of the state, and that all of the
others are either half measures, or backed by the Insurance
companies themselves.  The problem is that they all say this,
and therefore, somebody is a bold-faced liar.

The Cigarette Tax is another example.  The Occidental
Petroleum is a third.

In all of this, the people of this state, and in regards to
political candidates, the whole nation, are subjected to
lies, counter-lies, half-truths, and falsehoods.   It is
obvious that the majority of this advertising companies,
lobbyist, and political organizers care nothing for
integrity, honesty, or truth, and think even less of the
people of this country for feeding us such lies.

The people of this nation, by and large, are not ignorant
dupes, fools, or morons, and to be treated as such by these
organizations is the worst insult to what this nation is all
about.

I thank challenge you, and others in your body, to enact a
law to end this mockery of the American Ideal.  We have a
Truth in Advertising law which applies to products for the
public.  It is long past time that there was also a Truth in
Advertising law for political campaigning.  Political
advertisers, lobbyist, and those who present these ads and
bills should be forced by law to present only the truth, the
real facts, without glitter or embellishments, both good and
bad to the voters, and without the necessity of attacking
their opponents.  When you spend more time in attacking the

character of the opposition and less time on the real, viable
issues at hand, the voters see this as weak, vindictive, and
petty, and unworthy of someone wishing to hold office. It is
no different with these bills, and those who back them and
present them.

And this law should have teeth in it. If caught violating
this law, the offender should be banned for any future
involvement in politics, no matter what the level, serve a
term in prison, and be heavily fined, with the money going to
some worthy cause, such as medical research or the like.

It is obvious that these people will not voluntarily toe the
line in this matter. There very nature, and what they have
been practicing in the past shows that they have no
conscious, morals, or care for this country or the people who
reside here. They only care for their pocketbook and the
best way to take from others, what justly belongs to the
others, by foul means more than fair.

Granted, not all in public office are guilty of this shameful
behavior, and not all people in political advertising and
campaigning are without morals, conscious or scruples. But
they are in the minority, and are completely overshadowed by
the more base creatures who mar and deface the national
character by their actions.

At times, I think there should be a place in the ballot,
below the candidates names, and under the initiatives that
reads "None Of the Above", so that those in office can see
what the people really think. That way, if None Of The Above
wins, there would have to be a new election, with all new
candidates.

But again, I challenge you to push to create a Truth in
Political Advertising Bill, with appropriate penalties for
the offenders to give it some teeth. There is a joke to the
effect that Honesty in Government, like Jumbo Shrimp and
Military Intelligence are Aximorons. Problem is that they
are not jokes, and the Government and the Military have done
it to themselves. As such, it is up to them to clean up
their act before they completely loose the faith of the
people. And let not any in public office forget, We The
People are your employers, and with the current performance
appraisal, nobody deserves a raise, and some deserve to be
fired.

With disgust and a sorry heart at what the political machine
has become:

Michael Colin More

Michael Colin More
221 N. Cedar St. #33
Glendale, CA 91206

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS:
STATE CAPITOL, ROOM 5061
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 445-5976

DISTRICT OFFICES
401 NORTH BRAND
SUITE 424
GLENDALE, CA 91203
TELEPHONE (818) 247-7021

P.O. BOX 784
LANCASTER, CA 93534
TELEPHONE (805) 945-0566

California State Senate

COMMITTEES:
BANKING AND COMMERCE, VICE CHAIRMAN
ENERGY AND PUBLIC UTILITIES, VICE CHAIRMAN
SUBCOMMITTEE ON CABLE TELEVISION
BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS
LOCAL GOVERNMENT

JOINT COMMITTEES:
RULES
STATE'S ECONOMY

NEWTON R. RUSSELL
SENATOR, TWENTY-FIRST DISTRICT

MINORITY WHIP

OCT 07 1988

October 3, 1988




Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Action Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Calif. 95804


Dear Mr. Greenwood:

I recently received the attached correspondence from my constituent.
I would greatly appreciate it if you or a member of your staff will
prepare the following:

    X    A suggested reply for my signature.

    _____ An explanation of the problem described in the attached
           correspondence.

    _____ A review of your file to determine what assistance your
           office can provide.

    _____ A copy of your correspondence with my constituent on
           this subject.

    _____ Other:


I shall be awaiting your reply with the greatest of interest.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        NEWTON R. RUSSELL
                                        21st District

NOTE: Please reply to District office address.

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission is a nonpartisan body and therefore does not prepare correspondence on behalf of members of the Legislature or other elected officials. The Commission does provide information concerning the Political Reform Act (Government Code sections 81000-91015). The question presented by Michael More, your constituent, concerns false and misleading advertising, a subject not addressed by the Political Reform Act. Thus, we are unable to provide any specific answer about application of current law to Mr. More's concerns.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC can draft a response letter on behalf of a state senator addressing a constituent's complaints about false and misleading advertising in campaign materials and ballot initiatives.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to draft a response letter for Senator Russell because: (1) the FPPC does not prepare correspondence on behalf of elected officials as a nonpartisan body, and (2) false and misleading advertising is not governed by the Political Reform Act, which is the FPPC's jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request (Senator Russell's letter dated October 3, 1988) and FPPC response letter (dated October 24, 1988). Constituent's complaint letter from Michael More is also included. FPPC response is a declination letter. Minor OCR artifacts present in document text but key sections are clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC can draft a response letter on behalf of a state senator addressing a constituent's complaints about false and misleading advertising in campaign materials and ballot initiatives.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission is a nonpartisan body and therefore does not prepare correspondence on behalf of members of the Legislature or other elected officials. The Commission does provide information concerning the Political Reform Act (Government Code sections 81000-91015). The question presented by Michael More, your constituent, concerns false and misleading advertising, a subject not addressed by the Political Reform Act. Thus, we are unable to provide any specific answer about application of current law to Mr. More's concerns.

embedding.summary: Senator Newton Russell requested that the FPPC draft a response letter to a constituent complaint about false and misleading political advertising and campaign initiatives. The FPPC declined, citing its nonpartisan status and lack of jurisdiction over advertising matters under the Political Reform Act.
